title: Effect of Appeal on Execution of Order and Temporary Restraining Order
content: Under the 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs, the authority of the Secretary to issue a Temporary Restraining Order (TRO) pending an appeal has been removed. Instead, the submission of all the requirements for an appeal and the 60-day period to resolve the appeal automatically stay the execution of the Order. Therefore, there is no longer a need for the issuance of a TRO pending an appeal.

metadata.source_file: 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs Primer.pdf
